Name: EconomiYaki Capital
Description:
EconomiYaki is a term derived from the Japanese word "okonomiyaki" お好み焼き (a type of street food), and the Greek word "oikonomia" οικονομία (household management). 

Okonomiyaki gained popularity during and WWII around Osaka.
In a period characterised by scarcity and frugality, people could satisfy their hunger with a tasty and customised dish prepared with leftovers. Nowadays, it has become a signature dish of the Osaka prefecture.

We live by the values that this dish symbolises: maximize the value out of our modest financial resources.

Name: Stiftung Azubiwerk
Description:
Die gemeinnützige Stiftung Azubiwerk schafft bezahlbaren Wohnraum und bietet pädagogische Unterstützung für Auszubildende. 

Hohe Mietkosten erschweren es vielen jungen Menschen, während der Ausbildung in der Nähe ihres Ausbildungsortes zu wohnen. Besonders in Großstädten wie Hamburg fehlt bezahlbarer Wohnraum. Die Stiftung Azubiwerk setzt hier mit eigenen Wohnheimen an.

Seit 2016 betreibt die Stiftung mehrere Wohnanlagen für Auszubildende in Hamburg, darunter Standorte in Wandsbek, Harburg, Altona und im Münzviertel. Neben günstigen Mieten gehören Gemeinschaftsflächen und pädagogische Begleitung zum Wohnangebot.

Gemeinsam mit Förderern, Partnern und Unternehmen arbeiten wir daran, noch mehr Auszubildenden ein sicheres Zuhause zu bieten und die berufliche Ausbildung in Deutschland zu stärken.

Name: Talk with Death
Description:
I teach people facing serious illness to stop performing and start living.

When you're diagnosed with cancer, everyone expects you to be brave. Positive. Inspirational. Grateful for the lesson. You're supposed to fight, never give up, and document your journey for others.

It's exhausting. And it's a lie.

I don't do inspiration. I don't do platitudes. I teach a method I developed after my own diagnosis in 2021: talking with Death as an advisor, not an enemy.

This isn't metaphor. It's a structured psychological practice that transforms your relationship with mortality from abject fear into radical clarity. You stop performing the Brave Warrior, the Grateful Patient, the Inspirational Miracle. You discover what actually matters to you—not what you think you should want.

I'm a Master NLP Practitioner studying clinical psychoneuroimmunology. I work with founders, executives, and legacy builders who are done pretending and ready for truth.

Talk With Death is for people who refuse cancer archetypes, want to discover what they actually value, and build legacy from truth instead of obligation.

Description:
Mission 1
Medical Research and Application. We provide access to the best methods of medical care for those in need, those who otherwise would not have the opportunity to access the right treatment. We support and promote existing projects with Swiss and international hospitals / institutions dedicated to both research and active treatment of diseases.

Mission 2
Education and Social Life. Education for all ages is the most important factor for continuous development. From youths to students to adults, we enable education and training in all areas: for example, with scholarships or support for research projects and student start-ups.

Mission 3
Sports and Culture. We regard sports and culture as important factors for social development. Therefore, we support projects as well as individual athletes and artists. Here, too, our goal is to create the best foundations for the hopeful development and future of social interaction. In the cultural sector, the focus of the Leo Future Foundation is on theater, music, art and the support of projects and artists who share the goals of the Leo Future Foundation.

Mission 4
Environment and Sustainability. A flourishing environment is a crucial part of a promising future. The careful use of resources is essential to protect existing ecosystems. The Leo Future Foundation specifically promotes projects and institutions that positively influence sustainable development in our human habitat and in the environment generally. This also includes the transfer of knowledge and competencies in relation to sustainability and environmental issues.

Name: Miram Foundation
Description:
Our mission is to protect creative and scientific innovations, expand access to knowledge and learning, and promote equitable health outcomes for all — especially underserved and vulnerable populations. We believe that rights, knowledge, and health are foundational pillars of human dignity and sustainable development.

We work to:
• Advocate for fair and inclusive intellectual property frameworks that balance protection with public benefit.
• Build community awareness and capacity through research, workshops, and resources on rights, innovation, and access to information.
• Promote health literacy and equitable health services that strengthen community well-being.

Through strategic partnerships with policymakers, educational institutions, healthcare organizations, and grassroots movements, we strive to make systemic change that uplifts communities and ensures innovation serves the common good.

Join us in creating a world where ideas are protected, knowledge is accessible, and health is a universal right.

Name: Great Lakes Investment Fund
Description: The Great Lakes Mortgage Fund is a private investment fund offering bond opportunities to accredited investors, with returns ranging from 9.5% to 13.5% annually. We welcome serious investors looking to diversify their portfolio with a professionally managed real estate-backed bond strategy.
